reviews.review_text: Used to be playable, but I can no longer get into normal lobbies as a steam player, making this 20 dollar game into a useless icon on my home screen that is far to late to refund. This is ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥♥

This game is okay, but it gets boring pretty quickly. Don’t get me wrong, I do like it, but the “infection” gamemode starts to feel repetitive after like 2 - 5 rounds. The other “super” gamemodes also make it less fun sometimes since players can use stuff like long arms and other gadgets that kind of change the balance.

Casual mode is alright and probably the best way to meet people, but it’s often filled with little kids screaming, which makes it kinda annoying to be in one. 

The anti cheat is kinda annoying if your trying to mod in this game though, even if your alone in a private.

Overall I’d give it a 7.2/10. But $20 doesn’t really feel worth it for what you get, but it should probably be closer to $10–$15.

1/10 - I Am No Longer Allowed Near Children or Electronics
Gorilla Tag didn’t just disappoint me — it ended my bloodline’s legacy. I joined ONE public lobby, ten seconds in and I was being violated by a swarm of gremlins doing unspeakable war crimes while voice-chatting pure slurs, and immediately these horde of tiny demons were doing parkour on the ceiling, walls, and trees while blasting brainrot music directly into my eardrums. My arms are now classified as lethal weapons in three states. I tried to quit but the game followed me into my dreams and started calling me “unc” in a kids British accent. My mother asked why I was screaming “my name is anderdingus” in my sleep. 1 star. I fear the monkeys now. They know what I did.
